scenarios.description: String overload with empty fragment and null context returns empty node list (branch: fragment.isEmpty(), context==null)
pseudocode: String fragment = ""; Element context = null; String baseUri = "http://example.com";

scenarios.description: String overload with single element fragment and non-null context returns one node (branch: fragment non-empty, context!=null, single element)
scenarios.precondition: Document shell created for context; use Jsoup to create context Element
scenarios.expectedResult: returned list size is 1; node tagName is "span"
pseudocode: String fragment = "text"; Element context = new Document("http://x").body(); String baseUri = "http://x";

scenarios.description: Reader overload with multi-element fragment returns two nodes (branch: fragment non-empty, loop iterations N=2)
scenarios.expectedResult: returned list size is 2; first node tagName is "a", second is "b"
pseudocode: Reader fragment = new StringReader(""); Element context = null; String baseUri = "urn:test";
List<Node> result = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseFragmentInput(fragment, context, baseUri);

scenarios.description: String overload with null baseUri throws NullPointerException (branch: baseUri==null)
pseudocode:
String fragment = "

"; Element context = null; String baseUri = null;

scenarios.description: Reader overload with Reader throwing IOException wraps in UncheckedIOException (branch: Reader.ioException)
scenarios.precondition: Reader stub throws IOException on read; mocking necessary to simulate I/O error
pseudocode: Reader fragment = new Reader(){ public int read(char[] cbuf,int off,int len){ throw new IOException("fail"); } public void close(){} }; Element context = null; String baseUri = "x";
